citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care.
citations.narrative:
On November 3, 2025, registrant records show the facility self-reported an incident of two adult female Rhesus macaques
(“Macaque A” and “Macaque B” ) where twice daily oral corticosteroids were abruptly discontinued. On 26MAR2025, the
two macaques started treatment with prednisone for psoriasiform dermatitis. On 31AUG2025 prednisone treatment was
inadvertently ended without being tapered. A routine case review on 27AUG2025 finds registrant veterinary staff did not
recognize the medication was ending and erroneously did not extend the treatment. Thereafter beginning 04SEP2025
daily observations of “Macaque A” and her cage mate, “Macaque B”, show at least one of them had decreasing appetite.
On 07SEP2025 “Macaque A” was referred to veterinary staff for inappetence, hunching, and laying down with piloerection
while “Macaque B” exhibited normal behavior and appetite. An examination and diagnostics were performed by veterinary
staff which suspected “Macaque A”’s abnormal condition could be due to a variety of veterinary medical conditions. Based
on “Macaque A”’s status, the veterinary staff decided to euthanize her. Meanwhile, the other macaque, “Macaque B”, was
restarted on prednisone with no abnormalities documented for the animal’s daily observations during the lapse of
medication. Necropsy examination and diagnostics are inconclusive as to the cause of “Macaque A”’s critical presentation
on 07SEP2025. Abruptly discontinuing certain medications like corticosteroids may lead to the animal having symptoms
of pain, suffering, inappetence or death. Adequate veterinary care shall include the use of safe or appropriate treatments
which must be followed to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases. The registrant took immediate corrective action
to decrease the risk of inappropriate medication discontinuation in the future by establishing an improved system to alert
all staff when a medication must be reviewed or refilled. The facility must ensure that the use of appropriate methods to
prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries at all times. Corrected prior to inspection on 20JAN2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
Additional Inspectors:
Eduardo Vivas, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.33(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care. 
On November 3, 2025, registrant records show the facility self-reported an incident of two adult female Rhesus macaques 
(“Macaque A” and “Macaque B” ) where twice daily oral corticosteroids were abruptly discontinued. On 26MAR2025, the 
two macaques started treatment with prednisone for psoriasiform dermatitis. On 31AUG2025 prednisone treatment was 
inadvertently ended without being tapered. A routine case review on 27AUG2025 finds registrant veterinary staff did not 
recognize the medication was ending and erroneously did not extend the treatment. Thereafter beginning 04SEP2025 
daily observations of “Macaque A” and her cage mate, “Macaque B”, show at least one of them had decreasing appetite. 
On 07SEP2025 “Macaque A” was referred to veterinary staff for inappetence, hunching, and laying down with piloerection 
while “Macaque B” exhibited normal behavior and appetite. An examination and diagnostics were performed by veterinary 
staff which suspected “Macaque A”’s abnormal condition could be due to a variety of veterinary medical conditions. Based 
on “Macaque A”’s status, the veterinary staff decided to euthanize her. Meanwhile, the other macaque, “Macaque B”, was 
restarted on prednisone with no abnormalities documented for the animal’s daily observations during the lapse of 
medication. Necropsy examination and diagnostics are inconclusive as to the cause of “Macaque A”’s critical presentation 
on 07SEP2025. Abruptly discontinuing certain medications like corticosteroids may lead to the animal having symptoms 
of pain, suffering, inappetence or death. Adequate veterinary care shall include the use of safe or appropriate treatments 
which must be followed to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases. The registrant took immediate corrective action 
to decrease the risk of inappropriate medication discontinuation in the future by establishing an improved system to alert 
all staff when a medication must be reviewed or refilled. The facility must ensure that the use of appropriate methods to 
prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries at all times. Corrected prior to inspection on 20JAN2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Eduardo Vivas, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
